DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (TRANSACTION ID # 59339314)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAFAEL ORTIZ DBA ORTIZ JANITORIAL SERVICES' MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. IN MOVING UNDER CCP 877.6 FOR A GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT DETERMINATION, THE MOVING PARTY MUST SET FORTH THE VALUE OF THE CONSIDERATION PAID AND AN EVIDENTIARY BASIS FOR THAT VALUATION, AND MUST DEMONSTRATE THAT THE VALUATION WAS REACHED IN A SUFFICIENTLY ADVERSARIAL MANNER TO JUSTIFY THE PRESUMPTION THAT A REASONABLE VALUATION WAS REACHED. (FRANKLIN MINT CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2005) 130 CAL.APP.4TH 1550.) THE DECLARATION OF JULIET M. LOMPA, WHICH DEFENDANT RAFAEL ORTIZ SUBMITS WITH ITS MOTION, FAILS TO PRESENT AN EVIDENTIARY BASIS FOR THE PROPOSED $60,000 SETTLEMENT, WHICH NOT ONLY RELEASES ALL OF THE DEFENDANTS FROM PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS, BUT WOULD ALSO CUT OFF ANY LIABILITY OF MR. ORTIZ FROM DEFENDANTS PREFERRED BUILDING SERVICES, INC.'S AND CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S CLAIMS FOR INDEMNIFICATION. ATTORNEY DECLARATIONS LIKE THE ONE SUBMITTED BY MS. LOMPA ARE RARELY SUFFICIENT TO SATISFY THE REQUIRED EVIDENTIARY SHOWING OF A MOVING PARTY. (SEE WEIL AND BROWN BOOK AT 12:774). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-07-2016 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS PREFERRED BUILDING SERVICES, INC, CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is OFF CALENDAR per the request of the moving party.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
Off Calendar Jul-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT RAFAEL ORTIZ DBA ORTIZ JANITORIAL SERVICES' MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT is DENIED. In moving under CCP 877.6 for a good faith settlement determination, the moving party must set forth the value of the consideration paid and an evidentiary basis for that valuation, and must demonstrate that the valuation was reached in a sufficiently adversarial manner to justify the presumption that a reasonable valuation was reached. (Franklin Mint Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1550.) The declaration of Juliet M. Lompa, which Defendant Rafael Ortiz submits with its motion, fails to present an evidentiary basis for the proposed $60,000 settlement, which not only releases all of the defendants from plaintiff's claims, but would also cut off any liability of Mr. Ortiz from defendants Preferred Building Services, Inc.'s and Cushman & Wakefield of California, Inc.'s claims for indemnification. Attorney declarations like the one submitted by Ms. Lompa are rarely sufficient to satisfy the required evidentiary showing of a moving party. (See Weil and Brown book at 12:774). Prevailing party to prepare form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)
